cards.back: The Cartesian product of two sets A and B, denoted as A × B, is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a ∈ A and b ∈ B
A relation from set A to set B is a subset of the Cartesian product A × B, representing a connection or correspondence between elements of A and B

cards.front: What is the difference between a Function and a Relation?
cards.back: A function is a special type of relation where each element in the domain maps to exactly one element in the codomain, whereas a relation may have multiple outputs for a single input

cards.front: What is the Domain of a Relation?
cards.back: The domain of a relation from set A to set B is the set of all input elements, i.e., the first component of each ordered pair in the relation

cards.front: What is the Codomain of a Relation?
cards.back: The codomain of a relation from set A to set B is the set of all possible output elements, i.e., the second component of each ordered pair in the relation
